note: Illinois and Texas rates verified current for 2026 filing. California brackets are for income earned in 2025 (filed 2026) - the most recent full bracket table found; check for a 2026-specific inflation update before shipping.

IL.name: Illinois
IL.notes: Flat 4.95% on all taxable income, mandated by the state constitution. No standard deduction - uses a personal exemption (~$2,850-2,925 per person) instead. Does not tax Social Security, pensions, or retirement account withdrawals.

TX.name: Texas
TX.notes: No state income tax. Texas relies more heavily on sales tax and property tax to fund state and local government - worth showing as a contrast in the app rather than a blank row.

CA.name: California
CA.notes: Nine brackets from 1% to 12.3%, plus an additional 1% Behavioral Health Services surtax on income over $1,000,000. Standard deduction is much smaller than federal - roughly $5,540 single (2024 figure; confirm current year before shipping).
